content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA — STATE AND CONSUMER SERVICES AGENCY                    PETE WILSON, Governor

DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT & HOUSING
2014 T STREET, SUITE 210, SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
TDD (916) 739-4638




January 28, 1992


Ben Davidian, Chairman
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Davidian

This is a request for an opinion on the following question, pursuant to
Section 18320, Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Is a former employee of the Department of Fair Employment and Housing (DFEH),
who for compensation represents a public entity which formerly had a contract
for DFEH to provide services to it, entitled to possession of documents and
products developed by DFEH and used in providing services to the former
contractor? Further, would my action as a current State official in providing
such documents and products to the former State official constitute a
violation of Government Code Sections 87401 and 87406(d)?

I am Deputy Director for Enforcement (CEA level 2) of DFEH and, as an
additional responsibility, have been designated (pursuant to Section 8101,
California Code of Regulations, Title 2, Division 4) Administrator of the
Office of Compliance Programs (OCP).

Mr. Calvin McGee, who separated from State service on November 1, 1991
preceded me as Administrator of OCP and was my immediate subordinate while
exercising that role at the time of his separation.

Prior to Mr. McGee's separation, DFEH terminated a contract with the
Los Angeles County Transportation Commission (LACTC), a local government
entity. The contract called for DFEH to provide contract compliance functions
through OCP for that organization. The contract compliance function performed
for LACTC was similar to the primary function of OCP, which is to enforce the
Fair Employment and Housing Act provisions (Government Code Section 12990 as
interpreted by California Code of Regulations, Title 2, Division 4,
Section 8101) which require providers of goods and services to the State of
California to have nondiscrimination programs. The following is an excerpt
from the agreement between LACTC and DFEH (entire document enclosed for your
information) which summarizes the relationship:

"Specific duties (of DFEH) will include: monitoring of compliance,
receipt and review of contractor reports, preparation or reports and
statistical analyses, performing desk and field audits, processing and
investigation of complaints, preparation of findings of possible
violations, recommendations of corrective and remedial action where
appropriate...The Department will also train Commission personnel to
assist them in routine monitoring of contractor compliance with EEO
requirements."

Ben Davidian                         - 2 -                    January 28, 1992

The LACTC contract dated back to 1988, and the program was developed under
Mr. McGee's overall supervision during a period of time in which he was
reporting either to former Director Dorinda Henderson or to former Deputy
Director for Administration Austin Thompson. The Department was reimbursed
for personnel costs and direct expenses incurred in the process of
administering the contract compliance function for LACTC.

Prior to Mr. McGee's separation, DFEH had determined not to renew its contract
with LACTC, which expired November 1, 1991. This decision was brought about
because it was necessary to reorganize the Department to respond to
instructions from the Department of Finance to reduce General Fund
expenditures. Personnel support for OCP was to be drastically reduced because
of this requirement, and the Department was concentrating resources in its
core function of investigating and prosecuting complaints of discrimination.

Based on this reorganization, Mr. McGee was instructed on or about October 7,
1991 to advise LACTC that we would not be continuing our relationship beyond
the term of the existing contract. Subsequent to that decision, Mr. McGee
advised me that he was considering leaving State service and establishing a
private business which would provide services similar to those DFEH had
provided to LACTC. I advised him to take care not to violate FPPA
requirements, and provided him with some copies of statutory references.

On December 16, 1991 I received a letter from Kirk Rascoe, Manager of Contract
Compliance for LACTC. This letter noted that LACTC had "reached agreement
with The McGee Agency (TMA) to provide the services previously provided by
DFEH." It instructed me to "deliver to TMA all the Commission files, data and
materials which you now hold." Independent contact with TMA clarified that,
as a representative of LACTC, Mr. McGee was requesting copies of files, data
entered into our processing program, and a copy of the monitoring and
reporting system software used to manipulate the data. LACTC funds were used
to assist in developing that software.

Conversations with the Office of Information Technology indicate that there is
no bar to making a copy of the processing software available to LACTC. It is
not clear, however, whether a former State employee in the circumstances
outlined above represents LACTC is entitled to such possession under the
Fair Political Practices Act.

The concern which I have is whether my acceptance of Mr. McGee as an
entrepreneur exercising a role for LACTC which he formerly exercised for DFEH
involves the Department and/or me in a violation of the FPPA.

Sincerely,

[signature]

EARL E. SULLAWAY
Deputy Director
Enforcement Division

EES:sd

Enclosure

cc: Kirk Rascoe (LACTC)
    Calvin McGee (TMA)
    Nancy Gutierrez
    John Castello
